channel.title: Blogs on Harpreet Shergill Profile
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Harpreet Shergill Profile

item.title: Can AI Predict Crime Fairly? The Ethics Behind Predictive Policing
item.description:
🚔 Can AI Predict Crime Fairly?

“When we automate policing, we also automate the assumptions hidden in the data.”

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is increasingly shaping how decisions are made in modern society. From healthcare and banking to education and law enforcement, AI systems are now used to support or even replace human judgment. One of the most controversial applications is predictive policing, where algorithms analyze historical crime data to forecast where crimes are likely to occur.

item.title: Anonymous Course Survey Analysis: Achieving Insights Without Compromising Ethics
item.description:
Anonymous Course Survey Analysis: Achieving Insights Without Compromising Ethics

Organizations frequently collect feedback through post-course surveys to evaluate training effectiveness and identify opportunities for improvement.

In this case, participants complete a ten-question survey after finishing a course. Access to the survey requires authentication through organizational credentials, yet participants are informed that their responses will remain anonymous.

As the assigned data analyst, the challenge is not only to generate actionable insights for executive management but also to ensure that the analysis adheres to ethical principles, privacy requirements, and responsible data governance practices.
